dialectCatalog.google.gemini.generate_content: Google Gemini-style generateContent (varies by API surface)
dialectCatalog.github.copilot: GitHub Copilot — routable via proxy override (debug.overrideProxyUrl in github.copilot.advanced)
dialectCatalog.tabnine.proprietary: Tabnine proprietary protocol to Tabnine cloud/enterprise server
dialectCatalog.vertex.raw_predict: Google Vertex AI rawPredict pass-through API

notes: Proxy Override: sets github.copilot.advanced.debug.overrideProxyUrl (maps to internal.completionsUrl in Copilot Chat source) to route ALL Copilot REST traffic — inline completions and chat — through the gateway.

The debug.overrideProxyUrl key is a legacy internal setting confirmed in the official vscode-copilot-chat source (src/extension/completions-core/vscode-node/lib/src/config.ts). It is not a public API but is stable across current releases.

There is no user-configurable settings.json key for registering a custom BYOK model in Copilot Chat. Custom model providers require the VS Code languageModelChatProviders contribution point, which is not configurable via workspace settings.
Tier B rather than A because debug.overrideProxyUrl is an undocumented internal setting that may break in future releases; users should be aware it may change or be removed in future Copilot updates.

tlsVerification.notes: Copilot inherits VS Code's http.proxyStrictSSL setting. No extension-specific TLS toggle.

notes: Cline supports 'OpenAI Compatible' and also supports a Base URL for OpenAI provider.
Implementation should treat exact setting keys as discoverable by scanning contributes.configuration at runtime.
Cline is now multi-IDE: supports VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, JetBrains (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm), Antigravity, Zed, and Neovim (via ACP mode).

tlsVerification.notes: Cline relies on VS Code's http.proxyStrictSSL. No extension-specific TLS toggle.

notes: ⚠️ DEFUNCT: Roo Code shut down on May 15, 2026. The extension and documentation are no longer maintained. Community fork 'ZooCode' exists but uses a different extension ID.
Roo Code is a fork-family of the Cline-style agent and exposes explicit VS Code settings keys.
Exact keys may evolve; keep a scan-and-verify path.

tlsVerification.notes: Roo Code relies on VS Code's http.proxyStrictSSL. No extension-specific TLS toggle.

notes: Kilo Code supports OpenAI Base URL config.
Treat settings keys as hints; confirm via contributes.configuration in the installed extension.

tlsVerification.notes: No extension-specific TLS setting. Inherits VS Code's http.proxyStrictSSL.

notes: Continue uses a JSON config file (~/.continue/config.json) where OpenAI's base can be set via apiBase.
Your extension should back up the file and edit via JSON-aware tooling.
Continue.dev also supports config.yaml but the adapter currently targets config.json for backward compatibility.

tlsVerification.notes: Continue supports requestOptions.rejectUnauthorized in legacy config.json per-model. The newer config.yaml does not yet support this field; an upstream feature request is open.

notes: Claude Code supports LLM gateways through shared user settings environment variables used by both the CLI and VS Code extension (default ~/.claude/settings.json, or CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/settings.json when overridden).

This adapter writes ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from the active profile's stored secret, clears any stale ANTHROPIC_API_KEY value, and enables gateway model discovery for Anthropic Messages-compatible gateways before disabling the VS Code login prompt for third-party provider setups.
OpenAI Chat Completions endpoints are not directly supported by Claude Code unless the gateway translates them to Anthropic Messages, Bedrock InvokeModel, or Vertex rawPredict.
Gateway model discovery requires Claude Code v2.1.129 or later, runs only for Anthropic Messages gateways, and only adds models whose IDs begin with claude or anthropic.

When a profile is activated, the adapter rewrites the shared Claude settings file so ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN follows the active assigned profile; if no saved secret exists for that profile, the adapter clears the token and warns.
In the rare case an already running Claude Code session does not pick up the rewritten token immediately, restart Claude Code or VS Code.

tlsVerification.notes: Claude Code supports ANTHROPIC_DISABLE_TLS_VERIFY env var to disable TLS verification. No config file setting.

notes: Codex supports provider base_url and 'wire_api' (responses vs chat).
For AIdome: implement OpenAI-compatible Responses API for best fidelity.

tlsVerification.settingHint: CODEX_CA_CERTIFICATE or SSL_CERT_FILE (custom CA only; no disable toggle)
tlsVerification.notes: Codex CLI supports custom CA bundles via CODEX_CA_CERTIFICATE > SSL_CERT_FILE > system certs. There is no documented flag to disable TLS verification entirely.

notes: Official Gemini CLI does not present a general 'base_url' override.
Prefer tools that support OpenAI-compatible endpoints and let AIdome talk to Gemini upstream.

tlsVerification.notes: Gemini CLI has no documented mechanism to disable TLS verification or supply custom CA certificates.

notes: ⚠️ MARKETPLACE STATUS: CodeGPT.codegpt returned HTTP 404 on the VS Code Marketplace as of 2026-05-21. The extension may have been removed or renamed. Verify availability before relying on this adapter.
CodeGPT supports local providers and entering an API URL for Ollama/other OpenAI-compatible local servers.
Automation should either deep-link users into CodeGPT model panel steps, or set settings if keys exist and are discoverable.

tlsVerification.notes: CodeGPT has no extension-specific TLS toggle. Inherits VS Code's http.proxyStrictSSL.

notes: Not a VS Code assistant extension; treat as external app with setup guidance.
AnythingLLM supports a 'Generic OpenAI' provider pattern for OpenAI-compatible gateways via Base URL.
VS Code extension can include a 'Copy settings' step for AnythingLLM users.

tlsVerification.notes: AnythingLLM (Node.js-based) has no built-in TLS toggle. The NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED env var is the only workaround.

notes: Tabnine can be pointed to a Tabnine Enterprise Server URL, not an OpenAI-compatible 'base_url' switch.
Unless AIdome emulates Tabnine Enterprise protocol, treat as: detect + explain + guide.

tlsVerification.notes: Tabnine has no extension-specific TLS toggle. Relies on VS Code's http.proxyStrictSSL and NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS for custom CA trust.
